article_meta.title: Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 | President Donald Trump

transcript.content:
You are a news summarization assistant. Summarize the following article objectively and extract its key facts. Respond ONLY with a valid JSON object — no prose, no markdown fences:
{
  "summary": "string (150-200 words, neutral tone)",
  "key_facts": ["string","string","string","string","string"],
  "sources_represented": ["string"]
}
Rules:
- summary must be 150-200 words
- key_facts must contain EXACTLY 5 items — the 5 most objectively significant facts
- sources_represented must list every distinct perspective or source type included
- Do not editorialize, adopt the article's framing, or use loaded language
- Represent all perspectives present in the article proportionally

Summarize this article:

---
HEADLINE: Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 | President Donald Trump
SOURCE: Associated Press

ARTICLE:

Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says President Donald Trump's plane, Air Force One, returned to Joint Base Andrews about an hour after departing for Switzerland on Tuesday evening. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the decision to return was made after takeoff when the crew aboard Air Force One identified "a minor electrical issue" and, out of an abundance of caution, decided to turn around. Things are unloaded from Air Force One after the plane, carrying President Donald Trump to the World Economic Form in Davos, experienced a minor electrical issue after departure, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, and returned to Joint Base Andrews, Md. Trump will board a second plane to complete the trip. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) Members of the media exit Air Force One after the plane, carrying President Donald Trump to the World Economic Form in Davos, experienced a minor electrical issue after departure, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, and returned to Joint Base Andrews, Md. Trump will board a second plane to complete the trip. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) President Donald Trump boards Air Force One for a trip to attend the World Economic Form in Davos, Tuesday, Jan. 20, 2026, at Joint Base Andrews, Md. (AP Photo/Evan Vucci) Air Force One returns to Washington area due to minor electrical issue, White House says 1 of 4 President Donald Trump's plane, Air Force One, returned to Joint Base Andrews about an hour after departing for Switzerland on Tuesday evening. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the decision to return was made after takeoff when the crew aboard Air Force One identified "a minor electrical issue" and, out of an abundance of caution, decided to turn around. {
  "summary": "On January 20, 2026, President Donald Trump's aircraft, Air Force One, returned to Joint Base Andrews approximately an hour after departing for Switzerland. The White House indicated the plane's crew identified a minor electrical issue during the flight, prompting the decision to return as a precaution. The incident occurred as President Trump was en route to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos. Once back at Joint Base Andrews, unloading procedures took place, and members of the accompanying media also exited the aircraft. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt stated that President Trump would transfer to a second plane to continue his journey to Switzerland. No reports of injury or further complications were mentioned, and the White House emphasized the decision was made out of an abundance of caution.",

}

parsed_output.summary: On January 20, 2026, President Donald Trump's aircraft, Air Force One, returned to Joint Base Andrews approximately an hour after departing for Switzerland. The White House indicated the plane's crew identified a minor electrical issue during the flight, prompting the decision to return as a precaution. The incident occurred as President Trump was en route to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos. Once back at Joint Base Andrews, unloading procedures took place, and members of the accompanying media also exited the aircraft. White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt stated that President Trump would transfer to a second plane to continue his journey to Switzerland. No reports of injury or further complications were mentioned, and the White House emphasized the decision was made out of an abundance of caution.
key_facts: Air Force One carrying President Trump returned to Joint Base Andrews after departure due to a minor electrical issue.
The crew identified the issue approximately an hour into the flight to Switzerland.
The White House stated the decision to return was made out of an abundance of caution.
President Trump was scheduled to attend the World Economic Forum in Davos.
Trump will board a second plane to complete the trip to Switzerland.
